title: dodonaea divaricata
intro: Dodonaea divaricata, also known as the broad-leaved hop-bush or broad-leaved hopbush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is a shrub native to Australia and has been used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Dodonaea divaricata have been used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically or ingested to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
